title: Docstring claims default behavior removes track references; code does the opposite
description: The docstring says 'By default, this will also remove references to the removed media from tracks' but clear_tracks defaults to False. When clear_tracks=False and references exist, the function raises ValueError. The docstring describes the exact opposite of the actual default behavior.
expected: Docstring should say: 'By default, raises ValueError if references to the media exist on tracks. Set clear_tracks=True to remove them.'
actual: Docstring says 'By default, this will also remove references to the removed media from tracks'
verification_note: Confirmed: Docstring says "By default, this will also remove references" but clear_tracks defaults to False, and when False it raises ValueError if references exist. Repro script confirms.
resolution_note: Rewrote docstring to accurately describe default behavior (raises ValueError). Also added missing 'project' parameter to Args section.

title: Parameter 'project' missing from Args section
description: The function signature is remove_media(project, media_id, clear_tracks) but the Args section only documents media_id and clear_tracks. The first parameter 'project: Project' is undocumented.
expected: Args section should include: project: The Camtasia project containing the media.
actual: Args section lists only media_id and clear_tracks
verification_note: Confirmed: remove_media(project, media_id, clear_tracks) signature has project as first param but Args section only documents media_id and clear_tracks.

title: Docstring claims KeyError for invalid track_index; actual exception is IndexError
description: The Raises section says 'KeyError: Specified track ... can't be found' but proj.timeline.tracks[track_index] uses list indexing which raises IndexError for out-of-range indices, not KeyError. The KeyError claim is correct for media_id (MediaBin.__getitem__ raises KeyError) but wrong for track_index.
expected: Raises section should say: IndexError for invalid track_index, KeyError for invalid media_id.
actual: Raises section says 'KeyError: Specified track or media can't be found'
verification_note: Confirmed: add_media_to_track uses proj.timeline.tracks[track_index] which raises IndexError for invalid indices, not KeyError as documented. Repro script confirms.

title: Return type annotation says float but docstring says 'ticks' (which are int)
description: Effect.duration has return type 'float | None' but the docstring says 'Effect duration in ticks'. Throughout the codebase, ticks are integers (EDIT_RATE = 705600000). The actual value from _data.get('duration') is typically int. The type annotation should be 'int | None' to match the docstring's 'ticks' claim, or the docstring should note the value may be float.
expected: Return type int | None, or docstring noting the value may be float
actual: Return type float | None with docstring saying 'in ticks'
verification_note: Confirmed: Effect.duration at line 106 has return type float | None but docstring says "in ticks" which are integers. The _data.get("duration") typically returns int from JSON.

title: Missing Returns section despite returning BaseClip
description: add_media_to_track has return type BaseClip but the docstring has no Returns section. Users reading the docstring cannot determine what the function returns without reading the source.
expected: Returns: The newly created clip on the track.
verification_note: Confirmed: add_media_to_track returns BaseClip (return type annotation present) but docstring has no Returns section.

title: Malformed Sphinx deprecated directive — version number used as prose
description: The docstring uses '.. deprecated:: Use :meth:`move_to` instead (inherited from BaseClip).' but the Sphinx deprecated directive syntax requires a version number after '::'. The text 'Use :meth:...' will be interpreted as the version string, producing garbled output. Correct syntax: '.. deprecated:: 0.x\n   Use :meth:`move_to` instead.'
expected: .. deprecated:: <version>\n   Use :meth:`move_to` instead (inherited from BaseClip).
verification_note: Confirmed: Callout.position at line 381 uses ".. deprecated:: Use :meth:`move_to` instead" which is malformed Sphinx syntax. The version number field contains prose text.

title: Parameters 'clips' and 'fn' undocumented in apply_to_clips
description: apply_to_clips(clips, fn) has no Args section. Both parameters are undocumented. As a public API exported in __all__, it should document what clips and fn are.
expected: Args section documenting clips (iterable of BaseClip) and fn (callable applied to each clip)
verification_note: Confirmed: apply_to_clips at line 13 of batch.py has only a one-line docstring with no Args section for clips and fn parameters.

title: Parameters 'track' and 'fn' undocumented in apply_to_track
description: apply_to_track(track, fn) has no Args section. Both parameters are undocumented.
expected: Args section documenting track and fn
verification_note: Confirmed: apply_to_track at line 22 of batch.py has only a one-line docstring with no Args section.

title: Parameters 'timeline' and 'fn' undocumented in apply_to_all_tracks
description: apply_to_all_tracks(timeline, fn) has no Args section. Both parameters are undocumented.
expected: Args section documenting timeline and fn
verification_note: Confirmed: apply_to_all_tracks at line 28 of batch.py has only a one-line docstring with no Args section.

title: Parameters 'clips' and 'opacity' undocumented in set_opacity_all
description: set_opacity_all(clips, opacity) has no Args section.
expected: Args section documenting clips and opacity
verification_note: Confirmed: set_opacity_all at line 36 of batch.py has only a one-line docstring with no Args section.

title: Parameters 'clips', 'fade_in', 'fade_out' undocumented in fade_all
description: fade_all(clips, fade_in, fade_out) has no Args section.
expected: Args section documenting clips, fade_in, fade_out
verification_note: Confirmed: fade_all at line 41 of batch.py has only a one-line docstring with no Args section.

title: Missing Args section for ripple_insert
description: ripple_insert(track, position_seconds, duration_seconds) has a one-line docstring 'Shift all clips at or after position forward by duration.' but no Args section documenting its three parameters.
expected: Args section documenting track, position_seconds, duration_seconds
actual: Only a one-line summary, no Args
verification_note: Confirmed: ripple_insert at line 60 of layout.py has a two-line docstring with no Args section for track, position_seconds, duration_seconds.

title: Missing Args section for ripple_delete
description: ripple_delete(track, clip_id) has a one-line docstring 'Remove a clip and shift subsequent clips backward to close the gap.' but no Args section documenting its two parameters.
expected: Args section documenting track and clip_id
actual: Only a one-line summary, no Args
verification_note: Confirmed: ripple_delete at line 75 of layout.py has a one-line docstring with no Args section for track and clip_id.

title: Missing Args section for snap_to_grid
description: snap_to_grid(track, grid_seconds) has a docstring with a warning admonition but no Args section documenting its parameters.
expected: Args section documenting track and grid_seconds
verification_note: Confirmed: snap_to_grid at line 283 of layout.py has a docstring with a warning admonition but no Args section. However, snap_to_clip_edge nearby does have Args, so this is inconsistent.

title: Docstring for rescale_project omits side effects on transitions and markers
description: rescale_project mutates project_data in-place (documented) but also scales transitions and timeline markers (toc keyframes). The docstring only mentions 'all timing values' and 'scalar adjustment' but does not mention transitions or markers as affected data. Users may not realize markers are also rescaled.
expected: Docstring should mention that transitions and timeline markers are also scaled.
actual: Docstring says 'Scale all timing values' without specifying transitions and markers
verification_note: Confirmed: rescale_project docstring says "Scale all timing values" but does not mention transitions or timeline markers (toc keyframes) which are also scaled in the implementation.

title: Docstring does not document what 'stitched' means or the return type fully
description: auto_stitch_on_track returns 'List of stitched clips produced' but doesn't explain that these are StitchedMedia clips created by track.stitch_adjacent(). A user unfamiliar with the codebase wouldn't know what 'stitched clips' are from the docstring alone.
expected: Returns section should clarify these are StitchedMedia clips wrapping the merged segments
actual: Returns says 'List of stitched clips produced (one per group of adjacent same-source clips)'
verification_note: The docstring Returns section says "List of stitched clips produced (one per group of adjacent same-source clips)" which is reasonably descriptive. The claim that users would not understand "stitched clips" is subjective; the function name and docstring context make it clear enough.

title: Missing Args section for insert_clip_at
description: insert_clip_at(self, clip_type, source_id, position_seconds, duration_seconds) has a one-line docstring 'Insert a clip at a position, shifting subsequent clips forward.' followed by 'Combines add_clip with ripple_insert behavior.' but no Args section documenting its four parameters.
expected: Args section documenting clip_type, source_id, position_seconds, duration_seconds
actual: Only a summary line, no Args
verification_note: Confirmed: insert_clip_at at line 562 of track.py has a two-line docstring with no Args section for clip_type, source_id, position_seconds, duration_seconds.

title: Parameters 'x' and 'y' undocumented in move_to
description: move_to(self, x, y) docstring says 'Set the clip's canvas translation.' and documents the return but has no Args section for x and y.
expected: Args section documenting x (horizontal position) and y (vertical position)
verification_note: Confirmed: move_to(x, y) at line 1697 of base.py has a docstring with Returns but no Args section for x and y.

title: Parameter 'factor' undocumented in scale_to
description: scale_to(self, factor) docstring says 'Set uniform scale on both axes.' and documents the return but has no Args section for factor.
expected: Args section documenting factor (scale multiplier)
verification_note: Confirmed: scale_to(factor) at line 1707 of base.py has a docstring with Returns but no Args section for factor.

title: Docstring says 'Whether this clip is an audio clip' but implementation checks multiple types
description: The is_audio property docstring says 'Whether this clip is an audio clip' suggesting it only returns True for AMFile. But the implementation also returns True for UnifiedMedia with no video child, and for StitchedMedia containing only audio segments. The docstring is misleading — it should clarify the broader semantics.
expected: Docstring should say 'Whether this clip represents audio-only content (AMFile, audio-only UnifiedMedia, or audio-only StitchedMedia).'
actual: Docstring says 'Whether this clip is an audio clip.'
verification_note: Confirmed: is_audio docstring says "Whether this clip is an audio clip" but implementation also returns True for audio-only UnifiedMedia and audio-only StitchedMedia. The broader semantics are not reflected in the docstring.

title: pack_track clears transitions but docstring doesn't mention it
description: pack_track removes all transitions from the track (track._data['transitions'] = []) when clips are shifted, but the docstring doesn't mention this side effect. Users may be surprised that transitions disappear after packing.
expected: Docstring should note: 'Clears all transitions on the track when clips are repositioned.'
verification_note: Confirmed: pack_track clears transitions (track._data["transitions"] = []) when any_shifted is True, but the docstring does not mention this side effect.

title: ripple_move clears ALL transitions but docstring doesn't mention it
description: ripple_move unconditionally clears all transitions on the track (track._data['transitions'] = []) regardless of whether any clips were actually shifted. The docstring doesn't mention this side effect.
expected: Docstring should note: 'Clears all transitions on the track.'
verification_note: Confirmed: ripple_move unconditionally clears all transitions (track._data["transitions"] = []) at line 253, but the docstring does not mention this side effect.
